title: Witness Guard Plugin Specification
description: Technical specification for the automated witness key restoration plugin

witness_entry.description: A JSON array representing a witness configuration triplet

items.name: account_name
items.description: The name of the witness account to monitor

items.name: signing_wif
items.description: The WIF private key to be restored as the signing key

items.name: active_wif
items.description: The WIF private key with active authority to sign the update transaction

options.name: witness-guard-enabled
options.description: Global toggle for the plugin logic

options.name: witness-guard-witness
options.description: List of witness triplets in JSON format. Can be specified multiple times.

options.name: witness-guard-interval
options.description: Frequency of periodic checks measured in blocks (20 blocks ≈ 60 seconds)

external_options_read.name: enable-stale-production
external_options_read.description: Read from the shared witness config. When true, auto-restore is disabled until network participation reaches >= 33%, at which point the flag is auto-cleared.

check_and_restore.description: Core loop: validates preconditions and restores null signing keys

steps.action: Stale production guard: if enable-stale-production is active AND participation < 33% AND not emergency mode, skip all checks. If participation >= 33%, auto-clear the stale flag and continue.
Sync check: head_block_time must be within 2 * CHAIN_BLOCK_INTERVAL seconds of wall clock
Long fork safety: if Last Irreversible Block is older than 200 seconds, skip restoration
Expire stale pending-confirmation trackers (failed broadcasts) to allow retries
For each configured witness: look up witness object by name in witness_index
Clear any pending restore state for this witness

steps.condition: signing_key IS null AND no in-flight restore (or previous restore expired)

check_and_restore.returns: true if node was in sync and full check was performed, false otherwise

send_witness_update.description: Constructs, signs, and broadcasts a witness_update_operation to restore the signing key

steps.action: Build witness_update_operation: preserve current URL, set block_signing_key to configured public key
Create signed_transaction with 30-second expiration and head block reference
Sign the transaction with the configured active private key
Track transaction ID in _pending_confirmations and witness in _restore_pending

applied_block_handler.description: Signal handler connected to database.applied_block, runs on every new block

steps.action: Scan the block's transactions for any pending confirmation IDs; on match, mark witness restore as confirmed and clear tracking state
Look-ahead: if any monitored witness is scheduled in the next 3 slots, trigger an immediate check
Otherwise: if initial sync not yet done, probe every 10 blocks; else run periodic check every _check_interval blocks

startup_authority_validation.description: Runs at plugin_startup after the chain database is open

steps.action: For each configured witness, look up account_authority_object on chain
Verify the configured active key is present in the account's active key_auths
Log a warning if the key lacks authority; remove the witness from monitoring if the account does not exist

_witness_configs.description: Configured witnesses: maps witness account name to its signing and active key pair

_restore_pending.description: Witnesses with an in-flight restore transaction: maps witness name to the transaction's expiration time. Prevents duplicate broadcasts.

_pending_confirmations.description: Transaction IDs awaiting block inclusion: maps tx_id to (witness_name, expiration). Used to confirm restore transactions landed on-chain. Capped at 1000 entries.

_stale_production_config.description: Mirrors enable-stale-production from witness config. Auto-cleared to false when network participation reaches >= 33%, re-enabling key restoration.

_initial_check_done.description: Set to true once the node is confirmed in sync at startup. Until then, the plugin probes every 10 blocks instead of using the configured interval.

safety_guards.stale_production: When enable-stale-production=true, auto-restore is disabled to avoid broadcasting on a minority fork. Auto-cleared when participation >= 33% (same logic as witness plugin).
safety_guards.emergency_mode: During emergency consensus (dgp.emergency_consensus_active), the stale production guard is bypassed — key restoration may still be needed for recovery.
safety_guards.sync_check: Restoration only runs when head_block_time is within 2 * CHAIN_BLOCK_INTERVAL of wall clock time.
safety_guards.long_fork_detection: If the Last Irreversible Block is older than 200 seconds, restoration is skipped to avoid acting on a stale fork.
safety_guards.authority_validation: At startup, the plugin verifies the configured active key has authority on-chain; warns or removes witnesses that would fail.
